questions.stem: A dataset of house prices has a mean of £500,000 and a median of £280,000. What does this tell you?
options: The data is normally distributed
Most houses cost around £500,000
The data is right-skewed — a few very expensive houses are pulling the mean up
The dataset has errors
questions.explanation: When mean >> median, a few very high values are pulling the mean up (right skew). The median (£280,000) better represents the 'typical' house price here.

options: The middle value of a dataset
The most frequent value in a dataset
The average distance of values from the mean — how spread out the data is
The difference between the maximum and minimum values
questions.explanation: Standard deviation measures spread around the mean. A high stdev means values are widely scattered; a low stdev means they cluster tightly around the mean.

questions.stem: A correlation of 0.95 between variable X and Y means:
options: X causes Y
Changing X by 1 unit changes Y by 0.95 units
X and Y have a very strong positive linear relationship — they tend to increase together
95% of the variation in Y is explained by X
questions.explanation: Correlation of 0.95 means a very strong positive linear relationship. It does NOT mean causation, and the 0.95 is NOT a slope (that's regression coefficient) or R² (that would be 0.90).

options: There is a 3% chance the null hypothesis is true
There is a 97% probability the alternative hypothesis is correct
If the null hypothesis were true, the probability of observing this data (or more extreme) is 3%
The effect size is 0.03
questions.explanation: A p-value is P(data | H₀ is true). p=0.03 means: IF the null hypothesis were true, there's only 3% chance of seeing data this extreme. It does NOT give the probability that H₀ is true.

questions.stem: You have two class score distributions, both with mean=75 but stdev A=5 and stdev B=20. What does this tell you?
options: Class A and B performed identically
Class A is more consistent — most students near 75; Class B has wide variance — many students far from 75
Class B has more students
Class A has higher outliers
questions.explanation: Same mean, different spread. Class A (stdev=5) has tight clustering: ~68% of students score 70-80. Class B (stdev=20) has wide spread: many students scoring 55 or 95+. The mean alone is misleading.

questions.stem: What is the main advantage of using the bootstrap method for confidence intervals?
options: It is always more accurate than parametric methods
It requires no assumptions about the data's distribution
It requires less data
It runs faster than analytical methods
questions.explanation: Bootstrap resampling makes no assumptions about the underlying distribution. It works for any statistic (mean, median, correlation) even when the data is not normally distributed.
